sanskrit_devanagari:
योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रियः ।
सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते ॥ ७ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
yoga-yukto viśuddhātmā vijitātmā jitendriyaḥ |
sarva-bhūtātma-bhūtātmā kurvann api na lipyate || 7 ||

anvaya.meaning: whose Self is the Self of all beings

translations.verbatim_quote: One who works in devotion, who is a pure soul, and who controls his mind and senses is dear to everyone, and everyone is dear to him. Though always working, such a man is never entangled.
One who works in devotion, who is a pure soul, and who controls his mind and senses is dear to everyone, and everyone is dear to him. Though always working, such a man is never entangled.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 2040 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: One who is on the path of liberation by Kṛṣṇa consciousness is very dear to every living being, and every living being is dear to him. This is due to his Kṛṣṇa consciousness. Such a person cannot think of any living being as separate from Kṛṣṇa, just as the leaves and branches of a tree are not sepa…

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 5.7 When again, as a means to attain full enlightenment, this person becomes yoga-yuktah, endowed with yoga; visuddhatma, pure in mind; vijitatma, controlled in body; jitendriyah, a conqueror of the organs; and sarva-bhutatma-bhutatma, the Self of the selves of all beings-one whose Self (atma), the…
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-5-7-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara: the yoga-yukta is purified in the antaḥkaraṇa, has conquered the mind (vijitātmā), has subdued the senses, and recognises the Self as the Self of all beings. Such a one, though acting, is not bound by action. Shankara's emphasis: the four qualifications (yoga-yukta, viśuddhātmā, vijitātmā, jitendriya) plus the recognition of the universal Self produce the not-bound-by-action state. He notes this is an Advaita anchor — the Self of the yogin is identical with the Self of all beings.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 5.7 But a Karma Yogin remains engaged in the performance of pure actions prescribed by the Sastras, which are of the nature of propitiation of the Supreme Person. By this, he becomes purified in mind.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-5-7-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja: the karma-yogin whose mind is purified, who has conquered the inner sense, whose external senses are subdued, who looks upon the self of all beings as akin to his own self (in being a knower of the same form), even when acting is not entangled by action. For Ramanuja the 'sarva-bhūtātma-bhūtātmā' is read as similarity of nature among individual selves, not Advaita identity.

positions.rendering: dear to everyone, and everyone is dear to him (relational)
whose Self is identical with the Self of all beings (Advaita identity)
whose self looks upon the selves of all beings as akin to his own (similarity of nature)

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: Major sectarian disagreement. Shankara reads identity (one universal Self in all beings); Ramanuja reads similarity (the karma-yogin sees other individual selves as akin to his own); Prabhupada renders relationally as universal love. The engineering analog must respect this disagreement and not flatten it.

literal_meaning: The one joined to yoga, with self purified, mind conquered, senses controlled, whose self is the self of all beings — though acting, is not entangled.
traditional_meaning_consensus: Krishna describes the yogi who acts without bondage. Four qualifications: joined-to-yoga, purified, mind-conquered, sense-controlled. Plus the universal-Self recognition. Such a one acts (kurvan api) but is not stained (na lipyate). The verse pre-figures 18.66 and is the close cousin of 4.20 (ever-content, takes shelter in nothing, engaged-in-action does not act).
